seo.title: Direct vs OTA Bookings: Hotel Economics | Wink
seo.metaDescription: Direct vs OTA bookings on real economics: 5.5% all-in vs 15-25%, guest data, attribution and repeat value - with a worked $1M-hotel example.
seo.ogDescription: Compare direct bookings and OTA bookings across channel cost, guest data, cancellation behavior, offer control, attribution, payment flow, and repeat booking value.

fields.heading: Direct bookings vs OTA bookings: hotel economics explained
fields.body: Direct vs OTA bookings on real economics: 5.5% all-in vs 15-25%, guest data, attribution and repeat value - with a worked $1M-hotel example.

fields.heading: Choose the next step that fits your role.
fields.body: Direct vs OTA bookings on real economics: 5.5% all-in vs 15-25%, guest data, attribution and repeat value - with a worked $1M-hotel example.

cards.title: Subtract channel cost
cards.body: Include commission, campaign cost, Partner terms, platform cost, and payment cost where relevant.

cards.title: Adjust for cancellation risk
cards.body: Look at cancellation behavior by channel and by offer type.

cards.title: Add repeat value
cards.body: Include future direct relationship value where the hotel can communicate with the guest.

cards.title: OTA demand helps
cards.body: When the hotel needs marketplace reach, visibility in unfamiliar markets, or demand during weak periods.

cards.title: Direct demand helps
cards.body: When the hotel has a clear reason to book direct, good content, a strong Booking Engine, and measurable source attribution.

cards.title: Partner demand helps
cards.body: When a trusted recommendation can move a specific audience into a specific bookable offer.

cards.title: Platform demand helps
cards.body: When a bank, super app, loyalty program, or travel platform can embed hotel inventory with agreed terms.

items.question: Are direct bookings always better than OTA bookings?
items.answer: Not automatically - but the economics favour direct. A direct booking on Wink costs 5.5% all-in versus 15-25% on the OTAs, and the hotel keeps the guest email, so the next booking carries no channel cost. Compare net value: commission, cancellation behaviour, data ownership, and repeat potential.

items.question: Why do hotels still use OTAs?
items.answer: Reach. OTAs create comparison demand and visibility in markets where a hotel has little awareness. The goal is not to drop them - it is to stop paying 15-25% on guests the hotel could win directly.

items.question: What makes direct bookings more valuable?
items.answer: Lower cost and ownership. At 5.5% all-in versus 15-25%, a $1M-volume hotel keeps $95,000-$195,000 more per year - plus the guest email, the offer control, and the repeat booking the OTA would otherwise own.

items.question: How does Wink support direct booking economics?
items.answer: Wink runs one Booking Engine behind every channel - direct, social, partner, and Traveliko - at 5.5% all-in with Stripe included, and credits each booking to its source with 6-month attribution.

fields.heading: Ready to make hotel demand bookable?
fields.body: Start with the route that matches your team, then move into a focused demo or sign-up flow.
